project.name: DevStarter
project.description: A complete development workflow system for Claude Code and other AI tools — 83 AI agents, 51 slash commands, 30 SDLC runbooks, MCP configs, language rules, and templates, installed into ~/.claude/. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template for local secrets defining GitHub, Notion, and AI-provider credentials (Anthropic/OpenAI/Google) plus LiteLLM gateway and default-model settings.

nodes.name: .project.env
nodes.summary: Per-project workflow configuration declaring VCS type, GitHub repo, branch strategy (main/develop), PM tool, CI type, secrets backend, and AI provider for the DevStarter toolkit.

nodes.name: devstarter-config.yml
nodes.summary: Central DevStarter configuration mapping project, VCS, PM, CI, secrets, AI provider, per-tier model management, and team settings consumed by every workflow.

nodes.name: settings.json
nodes.summary: Claude Code settings controlling co-author attribution, tool permissions, the status line, enabled plugins, and dangerous-mode prompt skipping for this repo.

nodes.name: CHANGELOG.md
nodes.summary: Full release history of DevStarter from v1.0.0 through v5.6.0, documenting every feature, fix, and breaking change across 180 dated sections.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Primary project-context file every agent reads first: project facts, directory structure, Sanrio agent roster, VCS/PM/CI config, naming conventions, and workflow gate rules.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: User-facing overview of DevStarter covering what ships, install profiles, slash commands, agents, MCP configs, the 5-gate build process, and release flow.

nodes.name: USER.md
nodes.summary: Developer skill profile describing identity, overall level, per-language and per-framework skill ratings, communication preferences, and agent calibration rules.

nodes.name: devstarter-menu.md
nodes.summary: Entry-point launcher menu listing all DevStarter workflow options with instructions for Claude Code to route a numbered selection to the matching slash command.

nodes.name: devstarter-invoke.sh
nodes.summary: Universal workflow runner that lists available DevStarter workflows and prints the resolved prompt for a chosen workflow so any AI CLI can execute it.

nodes.name: install.sh
nodes.summary: One-command installer that copies DevStarter agents, skills, runbooks, and templates into the user's Claude Code home, supporting minimal/standard/full profiles and optional hooks.

nodes.name: setup.sh
nodes.summary: Interactive project bootstrap that verifies prerequisites, prompts for VCS/PM/AI choices, and generates the per-project config and skill-level calibration tables.

nodes.name: uninstall.sh
nodes.summary: Removes installed DevStarter files from the Claude Code home while preserving user customizations and custom agents.

nodes.name: update.sh
nodes.summary: Upgrades an existing DevStarter install to the latest version, migrating older layouts (e.g. commands/ to skills/) and propagating updated scripts.

nodes.name: list_workflows
nodes.summary: Prints the catalog of available DevStarter workflows the universal runner can invoke.

nodes.name: print_prompt
nodes.summary: Resolves and prints the full prompt text for a selected workflow so an external AI CLI can run it.

nodes.name: ask
nodes.summary: Interactive prompt helper that reads a user answer with a default fallback during project setup.

tour.title: What Is DevStarter
tour.description: Start with the README to learn what this project actually is: not an application, but a complete development-workflow toolkit you drop into your Claude Code home directory. It explains what ships (specialist AI agents, slash commands, SDLC runbooks, templates), the install profiles, and the 5-gate build process. Read this first to get the mental model the rest of the tour builds on.

tour.title: The Project Brain
tour.description: Every agent reads CLAUDE.md before doing anything. It is the single source of truth for project facts, directory structure, the Sanrio-themed agent roster, VCS/PM/CI configuration, the mandatory devstarter- naming convention, and the gate-approval workflow rules. Where the README sells the toolkit to a human, CLAUDE.md instructs the AI on how to behave inside this repo.

tour.title: The Entry Menu
tour.description: devstarter-menu.md is the launcher a user sees first at runtime. It lists every workflow as a numbered option and tells Claude Code how to route a selection to the matching slash command. Think of it as the front door: pick a number, and it dispatches you into one of the workflows you will meet in the next steps.

tour.title: The Specialist Agents
tour.description: The heart of DevStarter is its cast of specialist AI agents, each a markdown spec that defines an identity, scope, and output format. The Tech Lead (Tuxedo Sam) owns architecture, quality gates, and AI/LLM decisions; the Project Manager (Hello Kitty) drives sprint planning and PM-tool automation at gates 3-5. Reading two contrasting agent specs shows the pattern every other agent follows.

tour.title: Short Agent Aliases
tour.description: Building on Step 4, notice that each full agent also has a thin alias stub. agents/pm.md simply routes the short @pm handle to the full @devstarter-pm spec, and agents/techlead.md does the same for @techlead. This is a deliberate ergonomics pattern: users type the short name, and Claude Code resolves it to the heavyweight spec by filename.

tour.title: Language Rules Agents Apply
tour.description: The reviewer and resolver agents do not invent standards — they apply shared rule files under rules/devstarter/. The TypeScript and Python rule sets define the conventions (strict typing, error handling, testing) that the matching reviewer agents enforce. These high-fan-in files are the connective tissue between a language and the agent that polices it.

tour.title: Workflow Runbooks (SDLC)
tour.description: Each slash command is backed by a thick runbook in sdlc/. devstarter-change.md is the workhorse: it drives add-feature, remove-feature, and fix-bug flows through gated phases, and it is one of the most depended-upon runbooks in the graph. devstarter-starter.md handles greenfield projects. These files encode the actual step-by-step process the agents follow.

tour.title: Shipping and Tooling Setup
tour.description: Two more high-fan-in runbooks complete the lifecycle. devstarter-release.md defines what 'releasing' means here — bump VERSION, update CHANGELOG, merge develop to main, and tag — while devstarter-github.md and devstarter-mcp.md set up the version-control and Model-Context-Protocol plumbing that many other flows depend on.

tour.title: The MCP Configuration Hub
tour.description: templates/mcp/mcp-setup.md has the highest fan-out in the entire graph: it is the index that points to every individual MCP server config (GitHub, Postgres, SQLite, and more). When the devstarter-mcp runbook from Step 8 wires up tool servers, this is the catalog it reads from, with concrete config files like postgres.json doing the work.

tour.title: The Document Template Engine
tour.description: DevStarter agents emit polished HTML deliverables, and document-template.html is the shared base every generated doc inherits from — making it one of the most depended-upon files in the project. docs/index.html is the portal that indexes and links all generated documents. Together they explain how the toolkit turns agent output into a browsable project knowledge base.
tour.languageLesson: A shared HTML template acts like a base layout: each generated document fills in slots rather than redefining chrome, so a single edit to the template restyles every document at once.

tour.title: Installation and Hooks
tour.description: install.sh is the one-command installer that copies DevStarter into the AI provider's home directory and is referenced across the docs. The hook scripts go further: install-hooks.js merges DevStarter's automation hooks into .claude/settings.json while de-duplicating, and devstarter-invoke.sh is the universal runner that lets the same workflows work across AI providers.
tour.languageLesson: Claude Code hooks defined in settings.json run shell commands on tool events (e.g. after an edit), which is how DevStarter enforces formatting and guards without changing the agents themselves.

tour.title: Configuration and Live State
tour.description: Finally, the runtime config and state files tie everything together. .project.env holds VCS/PM/CI settings, devstarter-config.yml maps model tiers and project defaults, .claude/settings.json wires up the hooks from Step 11, and memory/progress.json is the checkpoint every agent reads at session start to resume work exactly where it left off.
